title: World Builder — Loading Maps from Files
description: Create a WorldLoader class that loads room definitions from a JSON file. The load_world(filepath) method reads the file and returns a dict mapping room names to Room objects. Also create a save_world(rooms, filepath) function that serializes rooms back to JSON. Include sample world data in the starter code.
test_code:
import json, os, tempfile
with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(mode='w', suffix='.json', delete=False) as f:
    json.dump(SAMPLE_WORLD, f)
    tmppath = f.name
loader = WorldLoader()
world = loader.load_world(tmppath)
assert isinstance(world, dict), 'load_world should return a dict'
assert len(world) == 4, f'Expected 4 rooms, got {len(world)}'
assert 'Entrance Hall' in world, 'Should contain Entrance Hall'
room = world['Entrance Hall']
assert isinstance(room, Room), f'Values should be Room objects, got {type(room).__name__}'
assert room.name == 'Entrance Hall', f"Room name should be 'Entrance Hall', got '{room.name}'"
assert room.exits.get('north') == 'Throne Room', 'Entrance Hall north exit should be Throne Room'
assert 'rusty key' in room.items, 'Entrance Hall should have rusty key'
os.unlink(tmppath)
out_path = tempfile.mktemp(suffix='.json')
save_world(world, out_path)
assert os.path.exists(out_path), 'save_world should create a file'
with open(out_path) as f:
    data = json.load(f)
assert isinstance(data, dict), 'Saved data should be a dict'
assert 'Entrance Hall' in data, 'Saved data should contain Entrance Hall'
os.unlink(out_path)
acceptance_criteria: WorldLoader.load_world(filepath) reads JSON and returns dict of Room objects
Each Room object has correct name, description, exits, and items
save_world(rooms, filepath) writes rooms to a JSON file
Round-trip: save then load produces equivalent data
hints: json.load(open(filepath)) reads the file into a Python dict
Loop over the dict items: for name, data in raw.items()
To save, convert each Room to a dict with description, exits, items
